PreToolUse.description: Block git hook-bypass flag to protect pre-commit, commit-msg, and pre-push hooks from being skipped

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
echo '[Hook] BLOCKED: Flutter dev server must run in tmux for log access' >&2
echo '[Hook] Use this command instead:' >&2
echo "[Hook] tmux new-session -d -s flutter 'flutter run'" >&2
echo '[Hook] Then: tmux attach -t flutter' >&2
exit 1

PreToolUse.description: Block Flutter dev server outside tmux - ensures you can access logs

PreToolUse.matcher: tool == "Bash" && tool_input.command matches "(flutter test|flutter build|dart run build_runner|dart pub|flutter pub)"

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
  echo '[Hook] Consider running in tmux for session persistence' >&2
  echo '[Hook] tmux new -s flutter  |  tmux attach -t flutter' >&2
fi
echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Reminder to use tmux for long-running Flutter/Dart commands
Auto-start dev servers in tmux with directory-based session names
Reminder to use tmux for long-running commands
Reminder before git push to review changes
Doc file warning: warn about non-standard documentation files (exit code 0; warns only)
Suggest manual compaction at logical intervals
Capture tool use observations for continuous learning
Optional InsAIts AI security monitor for Bash/Edit/Write flows. Enable with ECC_ENABLE_INSAITS=1. Requires: pip install insa-its
Capture governance events (secrets, policy violations, approval requests). Enable with ECC_GOVERNANCE_CAPTURE=1
Block modifications to linter/formatter config files. Steers agent to fix code instead of weakening configs.
Check MCP server health before MCP tool execution and block unhealthy MCP calls

hooks.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs');const path=require('path');const {spawnSync}=require('child_process');const raw=fs.readFileSync(0,'utf8');const rel=path.join('scripts','hooks','run-with-flags.js');const hasRunnerRoot=candidate=>{const value=typeof candidate==='string'?candidate.trim():'';return value.length>0&&fs.existsSync(path.join(path.resolve(value),rel));};const root=(()=>{const envRoot=process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT||'';if(hasRunnerRoot(envRoot))return path.resolve(envRoot.trim());const home=require('os').homedir();const claudeDir=path.join(home,'.claude');if(hasRunnerRoot(claudeDir))return claudeDir;for(const candidate of [path.join(claudeDir,'plugins','everything-claude-code'),path.join(claudeDir,'plugins','everything-claude-code@everything-claude-code'),path.join(claudeDir,'plugins','marketplace','everything-claude-code')]){if(hasRunnerRoot(candidate))return candidate;}try{const cacheBase=path.join(claudeDir,'plugins','cache','everything-claude-code');for(const org of fs.readdirSync(cacheBase,{withFileTypes:true})){if(!org.isDirectory())continue;for(const version of fs.readdirSync(path.join(cacheBase,org.name),{withFileTypes:true})){if(!version.isDirectory())continue;const candidate=path.join(cacheBase,org.name,version.name);if(hasRunnerRoot(candidate))return candidate;}}}catch{}return claudeDir;})();const script=path.join(root,rel);if(fs.existsSync(script)){const result=spawnSync(process.execPath,[script,'session:start','scripts/hooks/session-start.js','minimal,standard,strict'],{input:raw,encoding:'utf8',env:process.env,cwd:process.cwd(),timeout:30000});const stdout=typeof result.stdout==='string'?result.stdout:'';if(stdout)process.stdout.write(stdout);else process.stdout.write(raw);if(result.stderr)process.stderr.write(result.stderr);if(result.error||result.status===null||result.signal){const reason=result.error?result.error.message:(result.signal?'signal '+result.signal:'missing exit status');process.stderr.write('[SessionStart] ERROR: session-start hook failed: '+reason+String.fromCharCode(10));process.exit(1);}process.exit(Number.isInteger(result.status)?result.status:0);}process.stderr.write('[SessionStart] WARNING: could not resolve ECC plugin root; skipping session-start hook'+String.fromCharCode(10));process.stdout.write(raw);"

SessionStart.description: Load previous context and detect package manager on new session

PostToolUse.description: Log PR URL and provide review command after PR creation

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-format with dart format after editing Dart files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  if command -v dart >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    dart format "$file_path" 2>&1 | head -5 >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format Dart files with dart format after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Run dart analyze after editing Dart files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
  project_root="$dir"
  while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/pubspec.yaml" ]; do
    project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
  done
  
  if [ -f "$project_root/pubspec.yaml" ]; then
    cd "$project_root" && dart analyze "$file_path" 2>&1 | head -10 >&2 || true
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Run dart analyze after editing .dart files

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn about print() statements in edited Dart files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  print_stmts=$(grep -n "print(" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '//.*print(' | grep -v '_test.dart' || true)
  
  if [ -n "$print_stmts" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] WARNING: print() found in $file_path" >&2
    echo "$print_stmts" | head -5 >&2
    echo "[Hook] Use a logger instead of print() before committing" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Warn about print() statements in Dart files after edits
Example: async hook for build analysis (runs in background without blocking)

PostToolUseFailure.description: Track failed MCP tool calls, mark unhealthy servers, and attempt reconnect

Stop.description: Check for console.log in modified files after each response

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Final check for print() statements in modified Dart files
input=$(cat)

if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  modified_files=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.dart$' || true)
  
  if [ -n "$modified_files" ]; then
    has_print=false
    while IFS= read -r file; do
      if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        if grep -q 'print(' "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "[Hook] WARNING: print() found in $file" >&2
          has_print=true
        fi
      fi
    done <<< "$modified_files"
    
    if [ "$has_print" = true ]; then
      echo "[Hook] Remove print() statements before committing" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

Stop.description: Final audit for print() statements in modified Dart files before session ends
Persist session state after each response (Stop carries transcript_path)
Track token and cost metrics per session
Send macOS desktop notification with task summary when Claude responds
